verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 encodes a secreted cytokine with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or intrinsic outer-leaflet lipidation. After cleavage in the Golgi, the large latent complex (mature TGF-β1 + LAP + LTBP) is secreted and sequestered in the ECM. Although a LAP-bound form of TGF-β1 is presented on the surface of Tregs and platelets via non-covalent association with the transmembrane proteins GARP (LRRC32) and LRRC33, this surface localization is mediated entirely by those TM partners — not by any intrinsic mechanism of TGFB1 itself. This is reversible non-covalent recruitment ('something else holds it there'), which per the cardinal rule maps to secreted_only/no. Therapeutic antibodies against TGF-β1 target the soluble/ECM-deposited cytokine form (neutralizing mAbs), not a surface-integrated protein body.
